channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Oil prices turn lower after Iran says U.S. talks could be pursued based on national interests
item.description: The U.S. has bombed Iran for nine consecutive nights in retaliation for repeated attacks on oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack AI system to rival Nvidia, adding Microsoft as newest buyer
item.description: AMD is taking aim at Nvidia with Helios, its first AI rack system, and has landed Microsoft as a new customer, joining Meta, OpenAI and Oracle.

item.title: JetBlue wins Spirit slots at LaGuardia Airport, eyes move to Marine Air Terminal as airlines fight for space
item.description: JetBlue Airways won takeoff and landing slots for up to 12 roundtrip flights at LaGuardia Airport.

item.title: FIFA emerges as the $9 billion winner as the biggest, brashest World Cup ever draws to a close
item.description: The football governing body presided over the most lucrative sporting event in history and is poised to net over $9 billion in revenue for 2026.

item.title: There's an insurance name on Josh Brown's Best Stocks list that's been a homerun
item.description: Josh Brown and Sean Russo take a look at the insurance space.

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq are higher to start the week as U.S. oil wavers, chipmakers rise: Live updates
item.description: Stocks rose Monday as oil prices fluctuated in response to the latest bout of military exchanges between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Two injured after man with airsoft rifles lit fireworks outside NYC immigration court: MS NOW
item.description: The FBI told CNBC that an individual set off "an incendiary device" outside 26 Federal Plaza, which contains a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement office.

item.title: South Carolina Republicans crowd field to succeed Sen. Lindsey Graham
item.description: Rep. Russell Fry became the latest South Carolina Republican to launch a campaign to succeed the late Sen. Lindsey Graham.

item.title: Voters wary of government ownership in companies as Trump administration takes equity stakes
item.description: About half of voters believe it isn't appropriate for the U.S. government to own stakes in companies, a new CNBC poll found.

item.title: Alphabet stock pops on report it's developing a more efficient AI chip
item.description: The new AI chip, called "Frozen v2," would embed parts of Gemini's architecture directly into the silicon, according to the report.

item.title: World Cup audience stayed bipartisan despite Trump's prominent role, CNBC survey finds
item.description: Nearly half of registered voters watched the 2026 World Cup, with income and education shaping viewership more than party affiliation, the survey found.

item.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
item.description: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final.

item.title: Coinbase trading results may fall short of expectations, according to prediction markets
item.description: Speculators think in the company's second quarter earnings report, it will show trading volume fell for a third consecutive quarter.

item.title: Bezos backs CuspAI as startup teams up with Nvidia to hunt for chipmaking materials
item.description: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

item.title: UK's Andy Burnham becomes PM as Trump slams Britain as 'Poverty Stricken Disaster'
item.description: The prospect of Burnham, considered more left-leaning than Starmer, replacing the incumbent prime minister sent jitters through bond markets earlier this year.

item.title: FBI will no longer investigate ICE confrontations, The New York Times reports
item.description: The Department of Justice and the Department of Homeland Security have both denied that any change in oversight has taken place.

item.title: JPMorgan likes this missile and aircraft systems maker. Here's why
item.description: Moog is already up 61% in 2026, but it could see its stock trade even higher as its business gains more momentum across several industries, per JPMorgan.

item.title: Universal and Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sails to $124.5 million opening
item.description: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" tallied an estimated $124.5 million at the domestic box office, with IMAX screenings fueling a big haul.

item.title: Boeing CEO says company still needs 'a couple more years' to repair finances before new jet
item.description: CEO Kelly Ortberg signalled Boeing is focused more on stabilizing its existing business than rushing to develop a successor to its bestselling 737 MAX.

item.title: Ryanair says 'no shortage' of travelers despite Iran war denting profit
item.description: Ryanair's first-quarter profit took a 34% hit as consumers delayed bookings due to the Middle East crisis, while struggling airlines face a "difficult winter."

item.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Monday
item.description: Semiconductor stocks are higher, and Bristol Myers is buying new Nvidia chips for AI drug discovery.

item.title: How Social Security is calculated — and how to make sure you have enough money in retirement
item.description: Your Social Security check will depend on your lifetime earnings and when you claim. Learn how the Social Security Administration calculates your benefits.

item.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory build-out to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says
item.description: Speaking in an interview with CNBC, TSMC's Wendell Huang said the fresh investment comes on the back of robust customer demand.

item.title: FDA says Taylor Farms cyclospora finding was a false positive
item.description: The FDA on Saturday had said that a sample of shredded iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms tested positive for the parasitic illness.

item.title: China's car market heads for worst year since 2021 as sales plunge 20%
item.description: Consumer demand for cars in China tumbles following record-high sales of 23.7 million units in 2025.

item.title: From 'Super El Niño' to Europe’s extreme heatwave: How this year's weather shocks are rattling commodity markets
item.description: Markets are underestimating the impact of climate volatility on a range of asset classes, commodity strategists say.

item.title: 'Give me my money back': South Korean traders' leveraged SK Hynix, Samsung bets unravel after sell-off
item.description: South Korean retail investors suffer heavy losses after Samsung and SK Hynix shares reverse sharply.

item.title: U.S. Treasury yields edge higher as Wall Street monitors Middle East tensions
item.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — rose over 1 basis point to 4.558%.

item.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
item.description: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players, as well as formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

item.title: Russia pounds Kyiv as NATO military chief warns Moscow would 'lose a lot' by attacking Baltics
item.description: Russia and Ukraine traded their heaviest strikes in months, fueling fears of escalation and further strain on oil supplies.
